methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: An AI data center at megasite will create 2,000 permanent positions, Pittsylvania County officials promise
signals.signal_reading: A 2,000-job AI data center in Pittsylvania County appears distributed geographically, but data center operations require specialized technical depth that concentrates in existing metro talent pools—infrastructure without capability risks becoming shallow economic extraction rather than deep regional competency building.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals intentional community-driven capacity building beyond elite institutions, aiming for broad AI literacy and local innovation networks that could distribute deep capability if sustained beyond initial program launch.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia represents grassroots skill development in a historically underserved region, testing whether distributed peer learning can overcome geographic and infrastructure disadvantages to build genuine AI fluency.

signals.title: NoVA School Systems Grapple with AI’s Role in the Classroom - Northern Virginia Magazine
signals.signal_reading: NoVA schools grappling with AI in classrooms reveals platform-delivered tools arriving faster than pedagogical strategy, risking shallow consumption patterns where students use AI without understanding it—widespread access without depth of capability.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: AI pressure on Richmond's young workforce signals metro hubs experiencing competitive deepening of technical requirements, widening the skill gap between regions with concentrated tech employment and those without access to similar career pathways and training ecosystems.
